mechanism_narrative: A positive 6-month trend in PAYEMS signals expanding household income and corporate revenues, so higher payroll momentum drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value was 137699 thousand as of one month before 2014-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if higher 6-month PAYEMS growth has a nonpositive walk-forward Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over three consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month average by at least 25 basis points, the tightening signal raises equity discount rates and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above its trailing 12-month average by at least 0.25 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value was 0.07 percent as of one month before 2014-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of cases or if the mean triggered return is not below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth that exceeds CPI growth signals real consumption expansion rather than nominal price inflation, increasing earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value was 427186 million dollars as of one month before 2014-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if real retail-sales acceleration has Rank IC below 0.03 with 20-day returns over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A downward break in INDPRO below its 6-month moving average signals weakening production and revenue growth, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO crosses below its trailing 6-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value was 101.0251 as of two months before 2014-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO downside breaks are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in HOUST greater than 10% signals weaker construction demand and durable-goods spillovers, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value was 880 thousand units as of two months before 2014-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST declines do not lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns versus nontriggered periods by at least 50 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25%, markets price future policy normalization and margin pressure, driving negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises above its 12-month growth while FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value was 234.933 as of two months before 2014-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if this joint condition has a positive average 20-day S&P 500 return or a nonnegative Rank IC for three consecutive rolling years.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses below 7.0% from above, the labor-market recovery signal raises confidence in earnings durability and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value was 6.7 percent as of one month before 2014-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if below-7% crossing events are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in at least half of observed triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in RSAFS signals near-term consumer demand weakness and prompts short-horizon earnings-risk repricing, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value was 427186 million dollars as of one month before 2014-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if month-over-month RSAFS declines have positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or fail to underperform the unconditional 5-day mean.
